layer: Miss Lana / About, Shows hub, eight show pages, School Shows
description: Adapt the strongest owner-authored legacy content from magic-castle-puppet-theater.com into warm, vivid, modern English copy for Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre. Preserve the real plots, emotional ideas, moral themes, professional history, and theatre philosophy while removing obsolete brand facts, awkward legacy English, puppet-first positioning, unsupported superlatives, and outdated duration/contact claims. The output must feel like a welcoming children's theatre, not a technical company, SEO template, or generic AI-written brochure. Use the copy seed library in this task as the minimum narrative and tonal foundation; do not replace it with shorter, drier copy unless a documented factual conflict requires revision.
non_goals: Do not redesign page layouts, navigation, design tokens, cards, or imagery
Do not implement the Planning Your Event route, lead pipeline, analytics, redirects, or launch controls
Do not publish audience-size price tiers, fixed travel fees, discounts, or any price beyond 'From $350'
Do not turn the new brand back into a puppet-theatre brand
Do not erase a real puppet or mixed-format element from a specific production if the owner later confirms it; mark the format for confirmation instead
Do not invent plot events, characters, educational outcomes, awards, credits, dates, schools, reviews, or performer biographies
Do not resolve the final public titles of show #2, #5, or #6 without owner approval
Do not copy the legacy English verbatim when it is grammatically weak, outdated, culturally coded, or inconsistent with the new brand
Do not add setup, parking, power, weather, cancellation, or other technical information to individual show pages
Do not change business policies, contacts, duration canon, or service geography outside copy surfaces declared here

decision_basis.origin: User explicitly identified the legacy Magic Castle site as an owner-approved source of tested text and asked for a canonical TUNG that preserves its warmth because prior implementation copy was too dry.
investigation_findings: The legacy homepage contains a useful emotional philosophy: theatre impresses, delights, inspires, animates, and helps people feel and discover
The legacy repertoire contains plot and moral source material for eight productions
The legacy team section contains concrete professional-history leads for Svitlana Grygoryshyna, Armen Tadevosyan, Victoria Stolyarenko, and Roman Listopad
The legacy site also contains obsolete facts: Magic Castle branding, puppet-first category, 25 years, two public phones, 35–45 minute durations, and weak machine-like English
Current canon reframes the business as an English-first professional travelling live fairy-tale theatre with 30+ years of experience
Owner answers now support an approximately one-hour complete experience: roughly 30 minutes of story followed by roughly 30 minutes of games, dancing, bubbles, and interaction
The user requires the new site to remain light and emotional; technical information belongs on Planning Your Event rather than show pages

must_read: https://magic-castle-puppet-theater.com/ — legacy owner-authored source; authoritative only for plot, moral, philosophy, and historical leads, not current brand facts
Current implementation files for Home, About, Shows hub, School Shows, and all show-detail pages
must_not_violate: Owned public brand is Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre
Primary identity is professional live children's fairy-tale theatre, not generic entertainment and not puppet-first
English is the primary public language
Tone must remain warm, kind, imaginative, theatrical, and calm rather than loud, corporate, technical, or overexcited
The site must not use Russian/Slavic visual or SEO coding
Legacy content is a semantic source, not current operational canon
Current contact, duration, geography, pricing, form, and brand facts come from current canon, not the old site
Public price may only appear as 'From $350'
No review, award, biography, school, media-permission, vendor, insurance, or safety claim may be invented
Technical requirements must not be repeated on show pages
The exact format of each production — live, puppet, or hybrid — remains owner-confirmation-gated where unresolved

forbidden_execution_patterns: No mechanical rewrite that preserves the sentence structure of the weak legacy English
No generic AI filler such as 'an unforgettable magical experience for children of all ages'
No SEO-keyword stuffing or repetitive use of 'kind', 'kindness', 'magical', 'professional', or 'interactive'
No flattening of every show into the same template with only character names changed
No dry feature-list copy where a short scene, emotional promise, or story idea is appropriate
No fabricated plot detail added merely to make copy sound richer
No use of 'best', 'leading', 'premier', 'highest professional caliber', 'only', or similar unsupported superiority claims
No public use of 'East Slavic', 'Russian folk tale', Soviet references, or cultural-origin claims unless separately verified and intentionally approved
No claim that The Gingerbread Man is specifically an American folk tale without a verified content decision
No blind global replacement of Theatre/theater; preserve generic American search language where appropriate
No change to unresolved show titles in slugs, navigation, metadata, or headings without explicit owner approval
No publication of performer career details copied from the legacy site before names, roles, credits, and spellings are verified
execution_guardrails: Treat the copy seed library below as a floor for warmth and narrative richness, not as optional inspiration
For every page, preserve factual meaning first, then improve rhythm, imagery, clarity, and emotional flow
Each show must have its own emotional center, not just a generic moral label
Use concrete verbs and moments: follows, helps, hides, searches, shares, discovers, returns, celebrates
Prefer short paragraphs with varied sentence length over bullet-heavy corporate copy
Keep show cards concise; richer writing belongs on the detail page
Use a gentle moral framing: show what children notice or explore rather than lecturing them
Write for parents and educators, but keep a sense of wonder that reflects the child's experience
Avoid aggressive American party-entertainer language, excessive exclamation marks, all-caps urgency, and cartoon hype
Where a title or plot point is unresolved, keep a clearly marked owner-review placeholder instead of inventing a final answer
Where legacy and current canon conflict, current canon wins and the report must record the rejected legacy statement
Do not place operational details in story copy; use only a compact shared Show Details component and a link to Planning Your Event when available
If implementation space is too tight for the approved text, adjust layout carefully or escalate; do not silently compress the copy into lifeless fragments

readiness_notes: Current canon must already reflect approximately one-hour duration, current brand/contact facts, and From $350-only pricing
Current show route inventory and data model must be available
Show #2, #5, and #6 titles may remain provisional and must be visibly flagged for owner review in working artifacts, not necessarily on the public page

in_scope: Create a legacy-content provenance matrix covering philosophy, eight shows, and team-history leads
Prepare and implement warm English copy for the Shows hub and eight show-detail pages
Adapt one concise philosophy passage for Home and one richer philosophy/history passage for About
Use show-specific values to strengthen the School Shows page without turning it into a curriculum document
Create concise card copy and richer detail-page copy for each production
Create an owner-review sheet for unresolved titles, formats, plot details, and biography claims
Update current content inventory with verified adapted copy and source provenance
Add or update metadata descriptions only after the visible copy is approved and implemented
Preserve the current light page hierarchy and technical-detail separation
out_of_scope: Full team biography publication before verification

desired_effect: A parent should feel that a real theatre company is arriving, not a generic entertainer
A school director should understand the story value without reading educational jargon
A child-facing story should feel lively, safe, curious, and emotionally clear
The writing should retain the human warmth of the old site while sounding natural in modern English
sentence_rules: Use mostly 8–18 word sentences, mixed with occasional longer story sentences
Avoid three consecutive sentences with the same structure
Use one vivid image or action in each show introduction
Use no more than one exclamation mark per full page, preferably none
Avoid repeating the show title at the start of every paragraph
Avoid generic opening phrases such as 'Join us for' on every page
anti_dryness_test: Would this paragraph still make sense if every theatre name were replaced by a magician or bounce-house company? If yes, rewrite it
Does the paragraph contain a character, action, choice, feeling, or theatrical image? If not, enrich it
Does the copy merely list features? Convert at least one list into a natural sentence
Does every show sound identical? Restore its unique conflict, character energy, and emotional resolution

safe_to_use_as_semantic_source: Theatre as something that delights, inspires, awakens emotion, and helps people discover
Donkey's Birthday: friendship and mutual respect
Little Red Riding Hood working source: Wolf pursues a trusting girl; Hare understands the danger and helps
The Bunny's Little House: friendship and mutual assistance
The Magic Slipper / Cinderella: kindness is rewarded while selfishness is not
The Magic Castle: friendship, help, and wonder
Winter story working source: hard work and kindness overcome envy and laziness
The Gingerbread Man: caution, listening, and the limits of cleverness
Suzy Bee: a hardworking kind bee helps feed and care for friends
Names and broad role leads for Svitlana, Armen, Victoria, and Roman, subject to verification
must_not_transfer_as_current_fact: Magic Castle as the business brand
Puppet theater for children and youth as the global category
Middle and high school repertoire claim unless verified
Corporate events, anniversaries, and New Year's holidays as current service lines unless re-approved
Cultural-origin labels from the legacy site
Any unverified performer credit, film title, institution spelling, or role

copy_seed_library.usage_rule: These are authored working seeds to preserve warmth and narrative substance. They may be polished for factual accuracy, page rhythm, and final approved titles, but must not be reduced to generic one-line SEO summaries. The executor must record any material departure and why it was necessary.
copy_seed_library.home_philosophy_seed: Theatre has a special kind of magic. A story steps out of the book, familiar characters come close, and children are invited to feel, imagine, laugh, and discover something new together.
copy_seed_library.about_philosophy_seed: For Miss Lana, theatre has never been only a performance. It is a place where a child can meet courage, kindness, friendship, and wonder in a form they can see and feel. For more than 30 years, her work has brought fairy tales to life through actors, costumes, scenery, music, and the quiet magic of a shared story.
copy_seed_library.shows_hub_intro_seed: Every Miss Lana production begins with a familiar fairy-tale feeling and grows into a live shared adventure. Some stories are playful, some are tender, and some bring a little suspense — but each one returns to the values children understand deeply: friendship, courage, kindness, and helping one another.
copy_seed_library.school_values_seed: The stories give children more than a moment of entertainment. Through choices made by the characters, children see friendship in action, notice the consequences of selfishness, and discover how courage, patience, and helping one another can change a story.

show_seeds.card_seed: A warm birthday story about friendship, thoughtful surprises, and making someone feel truly loved.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: A birthday is coming, but the happiest surprise is not the cake or the presents. It is discovering how carefully good friends listen, remember, and come together for someone they love.
show_seeds.story_seed: Donkey's friends are preparing a celebration, and each of them brings a different idea of what will make the day special. Their plans lead to playful misunderstandings, small discoveries, and a joyful reminder that friendship grows through attention, respect, and the wish to make another person happy.
show_seeds.values_seed: Friendship, mutual respect, thoughtful giving, and celebrating together.
owner_review_required: Confirm the exact source book or remove all adaptation attribution
Confirm main characters and whether the birthday celebration plot matches the current production
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: A lively forest adventure where courage and a helpful friend can outsmart a very confident Wolf.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Little Red Riding Hood sets off with a trusting heart, while the Wolf is already planning a clever shortcut. But someone in the forest has noticed his tricks.
show_seeds.story_seed: When the Wolf turns his attention to Little Red Riding Hood and her grandmother, the quick-thinking Hare understands the danger. What follows is a playful chase filled with courage, warning, and help arriving at just the right moment.
show_seeds.values_seed: Courage, care for others, listening to warnings, and helping someone in danger.
owner_review_required: Confirm final public title
Confirm the Hare's exact role and ending
Remove any remaining Soviet 'Well, just you wait' reference
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: When Bunny loses a beloved home, loyal friends prove that no one has to face trouble alone.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Bunny's little house should be a safe and cheerful place. When that comfort is taken away, Bunny must find the courage to ask for help — and discover which friends will truly stand nearby.
show_seeds.story_seed: One by one, different characters try to help Bunny return home. Some arrive with big promises, while others bring patience, bravery, and the right idea. The story grows into a gentle adventure about loyalty and the strength friends find when they work together.
show_seeds.values_seed: Friendship, mutual help, courage, and standing beside someone in a difficult moment.
owner_review_required: Confirm final title: The Bunny's Little House / Bunny's Hut / another approved title
Confirm antagonist and helper characters
Legacy source called the production mixed and used puppets; confirm present format

show_seeds.card_seed: A gentle Cinderella story in which kindness opens a door that cruelty never could.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Cinderella's days are filled with work and unkind words, but she has not allowed bitterness to change her heart. Then one unexpected evening brings music, wonder, and a chance to be seen for who she truly is.
show_seeds.story_seed: With help from her fairy godmother, Cinderella reaches the ball and meets the Prince — but the magic cannot last forever. A lost slipper becomes the clue that leads him back to her, and the story reminds children that gentleness and courage have a strength selfishness cannot imitate.
show_seeds.values_seed: Kindness, hope, inner strength, and the consequences of selfishness.
owner_review_required: Confirm final title: Cinderella / The Magic Slipper
Confirm whether the father-death detail should appear publicly
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: A fairy-tale adventure where friendship and helping hands open the way to wonder.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Beyond the ordinary path stands a castle filled with questions, surprises, and a little bit of mystery. Reaching it is only the beginning.
show_seeds.story_seed: The characters discover that no one can solve every problem alone. Step by step, they listen, share what they know, and help one another move forward. The castle becomes more than a destination — it becomes a place where friendship reveals its own kind of magic.
show_seeds.values_seed: Friendship, cooperation, helping one another, and shared discovery.
owner_review_required: Confirm the actual plot and character list; the legacy source is too general
Confirm whether the show should retain the former brand name as its title
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: A snowy fairy tale where kindness and honest work bring warmth even on the coldest day.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Deep in the winter forest, every choice is noticed. A helpful heart finds friends among the animals, while envy and laziness make the cold feel colder.
show_seeds.story_seed: A hardworking girl treats the forest and its creatures with care. Her patience and generosity are rewarded by a mysterious winter spirit, while a selfish stepsister discovers that gifts cannot be demanded and kindness cannot be pretended.
show_seeds.values_seed: Kindness, hard work, patience, generosity, and the consequences of envy.
owner_review_required: Confirm final title and whether Morozko/Father Frost appears in the current production
Confirm exact characters and ending
Do not publish Russian or East Slavic origin framing
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: A quick little hero races through the world, certain that cleverness can solve everything.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: The Gingerbread Man has just come to life — and he is already on the run. Fast, cheerful, and very sure of himself, he believes no one can catch him.
show_seeds.story_seed: As he meets one character after another, the Gingerbread Man keeps trusting his speed and ignoring good advice. His lively journey brings laughter and suspense, while gently showing that confidence is useful only when it is joined by care, listening, and good judgment.
show_seeds.values_seed: Listening, caution, thoughtful choices, and understanding the limits of cleverness.
owner_review_required: Confirm final ending and how gently it is presented for young children
Do not call it an American folk tale without a separate verified decision
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

show_seeds.card_seed: A bright little bee works with care and discovers how much one helpful friend can change.
show_seeds.detail_opening_seed: Suzy Bee is small, busy, and always ready to notice when someone needs help. Her day begins with work, but soon becomes a story about sharing what she has with the friends around her.
show_seeds.story_seed: As Suzy gathers food and meets different friends, she learns that hard work feels even more meaningful when it brings comfort to others. Her kindness travels from one character to the next, turning an ordinary day into a gentle celebration of care and community.
show_seeds.values_seed: Kindness, helpfulness, hard work, sharing, and caring for a community.
owner_review_required: Confirm exact supporting characters and conflict
Confirm what Suzy is gathering and how she helps feed her friends
Confirm current live/puppet/hybrid format

about_team_leads.name: Svitlana Grygoryshyna
about_team_leads.safe_seed: Svitlana Grygoryshyna is the owner and director of Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre. Her work in children's theatre began more than 30 years ago and continues in California, where she leads the troupe and brings fairy-tale productions to families, preschools, and schools.
verify_before_expansion: Education, directing credits, festivals, awards, and film link

about_team_leads.name: Armen Tadevosyan
about_team_leads.safe_seed: Armen brings long professional experience as an actor, comedian, host, and writer. His background in stage, television, and live performance gives his characters strong timing, warmth, and an immediate connection with audiences.
verify_before_publication: Exact spelling and relevance of film and television credits

about_team_leads.name: Victoria Stolyarenko
about_team_leads.safe_seed: Victoria is an actress, writer, and creative theatre professional with training in drama directing and teaching. Her stage and screen experience supports the thoughtful, carefully shaped world of each production.

about_team_leads.name: Roman Listopad
about_team_leads.safe_seed: Roman is a stage and screen actor, theatre director, and acting teacher. His experience helps the troupe combine clear storytelling, character work, and a strong live connection with children.
verify_before_publication: Exact film, television, theatre, and commercial credits

steps.title: Capture and classify the legacy source
actions: Create a provenance matrix for every useful legacy passage: source meaning, current conflict, destination page, adaptation level, and owner-verification need
Separate plot/moral/philosophy source material from obsolete operational claims
Record all legacy statements rejected because current canon supersedes them
steps.expectation: The old site is preserved as a traceable content source without becoming current operational authority

steps.title: Audit current show copy against the seed library
actions: Compare current card and detail-page copy for all eight shows with the copy seed library
Identify dry, generic, repetitive, factually thin, or SEO-led passages
Identify where current implementation has invented or lost plot details
steps.expectation: Each show has a documented content gap and an approved narrative direction

steps.title: Prepare owner-review copy drafts
actions: Draft final card copy, detail opening, story synopsis, values paragraph, and compact experience line for each show
Use the copy seeds as the narrative floor while correcting any verified facts
Prepare a side-by-side owner-review sheet for unresolved titles, formats, characters, and endings
Do not implement unresolved factual claims as final public copy
steps.expectation: Eight distinct, warm, factual draft packages are ready for owner review

steps.title: Adapt Home, About, and School values copy
actions: Use the philosophy seeds to write one concise Home passage and one richer About passage
Use the school values seed and show-specific morals to strengthen School Shows
Keep these surfaces concise and avoid repeating the same wording across pages
steps.expectation: The wider site gains warmth and meaning without becoming text-heavy

steps.title: Implement approved copy
actions: Update shared show data and page content using owner-approved titles and factual corrections
Keep cards concise and place richer narrative on detail pages
Preserve one compact shared Show Details treatment and do not add technical logistics
Update metadata descriptions only after visible page copy is final
steps.expectation: The public site communicates eight distinct stories in a consistent but non-formulaic voice

steps.title: Run anti-dryness and factual review
actions: Apply every anti-dryness test in copy_style_contract to every changed page
Check each sentence against current canon, legacy provenance, and owner-approved review decisions
Review repetition across all eight pages and replace duplicated generic language
Record material deviations from the copy seeds
steps.expectation: The final copy is warm, specific, factual, and recognizably theatrical

steps.title: Verify implementation and close
actions: Run all required commands
Inspect all changed pages on mobile and desktop
Confirm no old operational facts, price tiers, cultural coding, or puppet-first global positioning leaked into public copy
Complete reviewer-grade closure and reports
steps.expectation: Copy, implementation, and documentation pass the full canonical closure

success_criteria: A legacy-content provenance matrix exists and clearly separates reusable meaning from obsolete facts
All eight show pages have distinct, warm, factual English copy grounded in the legacy source and current canon
Every show card remains concise while every detail page contains a real narrative opening, story synopsis, and values framing
Home and About use the theatre-philosophy source without copying weak legacy English
School Shows connects values to real stories without curriculum jargon
No public copy uses Magic Castle as the business brand, puppet-theatre as the global identity, 25 years, legacy phones, or legacy duration
No public page contains audience-size price tiers or any numeric price beyond From $350
No unresolved show title, production format, plot event, biography credit, or cultural-origin claim is silently invented
The eight show pages do not read like one repeated template
The final copy passes the anti-dryness tests and remains light enough for the existing page structure

required_commands: grep -RInE '25 years|35[–-]45|35[–-]50|highest professional caliber|Puppet theater for children and youth|East Slavic|American folk tale|\$400|\$500|\$600' app components lib docs/core || true
manual_checks: Read every changed page aloud; flag sentences that sound corporate, translated, repetitive, or mechanically generated
Run the anti-dryness test against Home, About, Shows hub, School Shows, and each show detail page
Compare each show plot with the provenance matrix and owner-review sheet
Confirm card copy and detail copy are not identical
Confirm no show page contains setup, parking, weather, power, cancellation, or policy details
Confirm Pricing remains From $350 only wherever pricing is referenced
Confirm generic American 'theater' search wording was not confused with the owned Theatre brand
Inspect mobile page length and rhythm; copy must not overwhelm imagery or CTA
Confirm unresolved #2, #5, and #6 title decisions are either approved or still clearly gated
Confirm team biography expansion was not published without verification
regression_targets: Light Home and show-page information architecture
Existing show routes and slugs unless separately approved

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm all steps, success_criteria, deliverables, source provenance, owner-gated decisions, and execution-safety constraints are complete
Step 2: Automated verification — run only the required_commands listed in this task and confirm they pass
Step 3: Reviewer-grade pre-closure review — review all copy and code changes for factual accuracy, tone, anti-dryness, repetition, accessibility, SEO restraint, responsive fit, and scope compliance
Step 4: Closure resolution — resolve or explicitly defer findings with severity; record material departures from the copy seed library and their factual/layout reasons
Step 5: PROJECT_PROGRESS update — append the final substantive completion entry only after all prior closure steps pass

post_actions: Do not merge unresolved show-title or format assumptions as final facts
Use FINAL_SHOW_COPY_PACK.md as the source for later media selection, metadata refinement, and review by Svitlana
Create a separate follow-up task if verified team biographies require substantial About-page expansion
Do not expand pricing beyond From $350 without a new owner decision and canon-sync task
